discrepancies.description: pypdf 6.10.2 silently substitutes the section-symbol '§' with the lowercase letter 'w' in the BT 1933 PDF text extraction. Section-headers such as '§ 4. A lemma on summation' render as 'w 4. A lemma on summation' in the raw pypdf output.
discrepancies.resolution: Section-symbol restored silently by hand-correction in the verbatim extract files (D2, D4, D6); restoration is uniform and lossless because the surrounding context is unambiguous (each occurrence is a section-header token followed by an integer + period + section-title).
discrepancies.scope: section-headers only; the substitution does not affect any in-body word.
discrepancies.impact: low; the verbatim extracts faithfully reflect the BT 1933 text post-restoration.

discrepancies.description: In the §6 body title, pypdf renders 'Factorization' as 'Faetorization' (single-character font-drift on the 'c' glyph). The §6 section-header at PDF p. 48 is correctly extracted as 'A LEMMA ON FACTORIZATION'; the body-text repeat at PDF p. 50 line 2 reads 'Faetorization'.
discrepancies.resolution: Documented as a discrepancy; not silently corrected in the §6 extract because the verbatim policy means we transcribe what pypdf delivered. The reader is alerted in `bt_1933_section_index_4_6.md` and in the `bt_1933_section_6_extract.md` introductory note.
